codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking gap: performKeyExchange now rejects revoked peers before ECDH/session persistence, and new tests cover the blocked key-exchange path and trust-boundary behavior. Comparing the original failed diff (0eba83d) and fix diff (114d804), the missing revocation check at key-exchange time is now enforced.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mors-global-worker skill requires explicit rationale when manual checks are skipped for internal-only changes, but this handoff marked followedProcedure=true without an explicit manual-checks-not-applicable note.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md steps 8 and 10 vs handoff 2026-02-28T14-15-58-524Z__global-async-fix-revocation-enforced-key-exchange__bb201382-90a0-44e3-b5d0-1015773be5ef.json (verification includes commandsRun only, no interactiveChecks/rationale).

summary: Pass. The original blocking issue (no revocation gate in performKeyExchange) is fixed in commit 114d804, and the new revocation-at-key-exchange tests demonstrate revoked peers cannot re-establish trust via fresh exchange.
